title: `podman kube play` should warn on unsupported kind
body:
### Feature request description

When running `podman kube play` on a YAML file containing unsupported resources (E.g. kind: Service) we do not get any warnings. Which can lead to situation where we expect something but is not available !







### Suggest potential solution

When running `podman kube play` we get a `PlayKubeReport` as response

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/3f85181382700016df007d773be23c708b1903f4/pkg/domain/entities/types/play.go#L22-L36

We could add a `Warnings: []string` in the report, this report could be populate with a warning every time we hit an unsupported kind. 

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/8ce77d6e6b33c57ec47ed1ad1c8d624fb661c919/pkg/domain/infra/abi/play.go#L352-L353

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/8ce77d6e6b33c57ec47ed1ad1c8d624fb661c919/pkg/domain/infra/abi/play.go#L479-L481

### Have you considered any alternatives?

No

### Additional context

Might be helpful for https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/18332

---

I am interested to work on this feature

comments.body: you can assign this to yourself by adding a comment of `assign` with a preceding forward slash.

As of today, running `podman kube play` with only unsupported types will make the command fails

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/f66f7c8a5b142f2452a61eced63e16d0ca30b4e7/pkg/domain/infra/abi/play.go#L485-L490

The original proposal in the issue was to make a warning if some kind were unsupported, but wouldn't it make sense to just fail if there is any unsupported kind?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Tend to agree we should fail on unsupported type. Arguably a breaking change, so it would be nice to get this in 6.0.
